scenario: Apex Healthcare runs Camunda 8.8 SaaS with 7400 instances per day across its patient onboarding flow. A platform engineer is reviewing which BPMN elements the Operate migration view will let the operator map from source to target on the cluster, and is checking the documented constraint that governs this capability.
question: Which constraint governs which BPMN elements can be mapped in the Operate migration view?

options.text: Only elements decorated with the camunda:migratable extension can be mapped from source to target in the migration view of Operate on the affected cluster setup
Only elements that share the same BPMN id between source and target versions can be mapped from source to target in the migration view of Operate across the cluster
Only elements with migration supported by Zeebe can be mapped from source to target in the Operate migration view, per the documented supported elements list
Only elements deployed within the same tenant as the source process version can be mapped from source to target in the migration view of Operate across the cluster

a.text: Incorrect. There is no camunda:migratable extension attribute; the documented constraint is that only elements with migration supported by Zeebe can be mapped in the migration view.

b.text: Incorrect. Element BPMN ids do not have to match across versions; the documented gate is whether the element has migration supported by Zeebe, not id equality between source and target.

c.text: Correct. The migration guide says it is currently only possible to map elements with migration supported by Zeebe, and links out to the list of supported BPMN elements that can be mapped.

d.text: Incorrect. The constraint is not tenant scoping; it is element type support. Mapping is gated by whether the BPMN element has migration support in Zeebe, not by tenant boundaries.

explanation: Per the migration guide, mapping in the migration view is currently only possible for BPMN elements with migration supported by Zeebe, and the supported elements list is referenced from the migration concepts documentation.
